chunks.text: Jonathan Shewchuk is a Professor in the Computer Science Division of the Department of Electrical Engineering and Computer Sciences at UC Berkeley. His contact information includes: 529 Soda Hall, telephone 510-642-3936, fax 510-642-3962, email jrs@cs.berkeley.edu. Administrative support is provided by Monica Naputi (mnaputi@berkeley.edu).
chunks.question: What is Jonathan Shewchuk's position and contact details?
chunks.answer: Professor at UC Berkeley with Soda Hall office.

chunks.text: Jonathan Shewchuk earned a B.Sc. in Physics and Computing Science from Simon Fraser University in 1990, followed by M.S. and Ph.D. degrees in Computer Science from Carnegie Mellon University, completing his Ph.D. in 1997. He joined UC Berkeley's Computer Science Division in 1998.
chunks.question: What are Jonathan Shewchuk's educational background and career timeline?
chunks.answer: B.Sc. from Simon Fraser, Ph.D. from CMU, joined Berkeley 1998.

chunks.text: Jonathan Shewchuk's research areas include Scientific Computing (SCI), Theory (THY), and Graphics (GR). He is associated with the Visual Computing Lab (VCL) research center. His teaching includes CS 274: Computational Geometry, held on Mondays and Wednesdays from 14:30-15:59 in Soda 320.
chunks.question: What are Shewchuk's research areas and current teaching?
chunks.answer: Scientific Computing, Theory, Graphics; teaches Computational Geometry.

chunks.text: Selected publications by Jonathan Shewchuk include: 'Unstructured Mesh Generation' in Combinatorial Scientific Computing (2012), 'Updated Sparse Cholesky Factors for Corotational Elastodynamics' in ACM Transactions on Graphics (2012), and 'General-dimensional constrained Delaunay triangulations and constrained regular triangulations I' in Discrete & Computational Geometry (2008).
chunks.question: What are some key publications by Jonathan Shewchuk?
chunks.answer: Works on mesh generation, triangulations, and sparse Cholesky factors.

chunks.text: Additional selected publications include: 'Star splaying: An algorithm for repairing Delaunay triangulations and convex hulls' (2005), 'Interpolating and approximating implicit surfaces from polygon soup' (2004), 'Anisotropic Voronoi diagrams and guaranteed-quality anisotropic mesh generation' (2003), and 'Triangle: Engineering a 2D quality mesh generator and Delaunay triangulator' (1996).
chunks.question: What algorithms and methods has Shewchuk published?
chunks.answer: Algorithms for triangulation, mesh generation, and surface approximation.

chunks.text: Jonathan Shewchuk has received several honors and awards: Diane S. McEntyre Award for Excellence in Teaching Computer Science (2010), Jim and Donna Gray Award for Excellence in Undergraduate Teaching of Computer Science (2006), Sloan Research Fellow (2004), James H. Wilkinson Prize for Numerical Software (2003), Okawa Research Grant (1999), and NSF Faculty Early Career Development Award (CAREER) (1999).
chunks.question: What awards has Jonathan Shewchuk received?
chunks.answer: Teaching awards, Sloan Fellowship, Wilkinson Prize, and NSF CAREER.
